topic: Sharp non-equivalence theorem for local-projection (LP) versus structural-vector-autoregression (SVAR) identification of structural impulse responses under non-Gaussian innovations and partial sign restrictions.

open_problems.open_problem: Characterize primitive fourth-cumulant and partial sign/zero conditions that imply a positive LP/sign versus non-Gaussian SVAR/sign support-function gap on an open class of VAR laws.

source_refs.claim: LPs and VARs estimate the same population impulse responses when the same identifying restrictions are imposed, including sign restrictions.
source_refs.author: Mikkel Plagborg-Moller and Christian K. Wolf

source_refs.claim: Independence can refine sign-restricted VAR identified sets, but weak higher-moment information may leave nonconvex and only partially tightened impulse-response sets.
source_refs.author: Thorsten Drautzburg and Jonathan H. Wright

source_refs.claim: Independent non-Gaussian structural errors can make SVAR impulse responses essentially unique, with Gaussianity as the non-identification exception.
source_refs.author: Markku Lanne, Mika Meitz, and Pentti Saikkonen

source_refs.excerpt: no axis-b non-equivalence threshold derived in closed form
prove Assumption~\ref{ass:quant-margin} from the primitive fourth-cumulant geometry

open_problems.why_unsolved: The published LP/VAR equivalence result is explicitly a common-restriction result, while the non-Gaussian SVAR papers use higher-order shock-law information to restrict rotations. The v1 CausalSmith proposal only obtained strict separation after assuming a positive quantitative margin, and reviewers flagged that the primitive open-class separation from cumulant tensor geometry and partial signs was still unproved.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A sufficient-and-sharp support-function theorem giving primitive semi-algebraic conditions under which the LP/sign support maximizer is separated from the cumulant-diagonalizing SVAR locus.

open_problems.open_problem: Determine when horizonwise LP sign/zero-admissible response rows paste into one common rotation that diagonalizes the observed fourth-cumulant tensor for a labelled non-Gaussian SVAR shock.

source_refs.claim: ICA-style non-Gaussian restrictions identify SVAR structural shocks through one innovation system rather than horizon-by-horizon response inequalities.
source_refs.author: Christian Gourieroux, Alain Monfort, and Jean-Paul Renne

source_refs.claim: Sign and zero restrictions define SVAR feasible rotations and inference problems, but do not add non-Gaussian cumulant diagonalization.
source_refs.author: Jonas E. Arias, Juan F. Rubio-Ramirez, and Daniel F. Waggoner

source_refs.excerpt: when do response rows satisfying the LP sign pattern paste into one fourth-cumulant diagonalizing rotation
requiring the same impact rotation to diagonalize the observed fourth cumulant tensor

open_problems.why_unsolved: The sign/zero SVAR literature gives rotation geometry and the non-Gaussian literature gives one-law cumulant or ICA restrictions, but the exact pasting boundary between horizonwise LP response feasibility and one common structural shock law is not stated. The prior proposal identified this common-rotation issue but left it as a boundary condition rather than an iff characterization.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: An algebraic compatibility criterion, ideally necessary and sufficient, for LP response rows, partial sign/zero inequalities, and a single cumulant-diagonalizing orthogonal matrix.

open_problems.open_problem: Construct a computable cumulant-matching diagnostic that lower-bounds the LP/SVAR support-function gap under partial sign restrictions.

source_refs.claim: Higher unconditional moments yield local SVAR identification rank conditions and tests, including cases where only subsets of structural parameters are identified.
source_refs.author: Alain Guay

source_refs.claim: Sign/zero restriction algorithms decide nonemptiness and generate admissible rotations, but do not attach a non-Gaussian cumulant-distance support bound.
source_refs.author: Matthew Read

source_refs.excerpt: a computable cumulant-matching diagnostic with a quantitative support-gap lower bound
rank/transversality condition ensuring the LP support argmax is separated

open_problems.why_unsolved: Existing higher-moment papers provide rank or identification checks and sign/zero papers provide feasible-rotation algorithms, but neither gives a quantitative distance from an LP support optimizer to the cumulant-diagonalizing locus. CausalSmith reviewers explicitly required a diagnostic and lower bound because generic strict inclusion alone was too close to constraint-omission bookkeeping.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A theorem turning a tensor-diagonalization residual, rank gap, or transversality distance into an explicit positive lower bound on h_LP(w;P)-h_SVAR(w;P).

open_problems.open_problem: Characterize the boundary where partial sign/zero restrictions only label non-Gaussian SVAR components versus where they conflict with or shrink the cumulant-identified structural set.

source_refs.claim: Non-Gaussianity of potentially dependent shocks can identify some shocks while leaving others set identified through orthogonal blocks.
source_refs.author: Markku Lanne, Keyan Liu, and Jani Luoto

source_refs.claim: Adding independence to sign restrictions can reject many rotations and produce nonconvex impulse-response confidence sets.
source_refs.author: Thorsten Drautzburg and Jonathan H. Wright

source_refs.excerpt: neither gives a horizonwise-LP versus common-SVAR completion boundary
equal kurtoses showing the diagonalizer is not unique

open_problems.why_unsolved: Near equal-kurtosis or dependent-shock regimes, higher moments may identify only a subspace or an orthogonal block rather than a unique shock. Sign/zero restrictions can then act as labels, refinements, or inconsistent extra constraints, and the published literature does not give the sharp LP/SVAR response-set transition for these cases.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A sharp identified-set theorem classifying label-only, shrinkage, conflict, and equal-kurtosis boundary regimes for partial signs combined with non-Gaussian cumulant restrictions.

open_problems.open_problem: Upgrade the two-shock closed-form positive-gap witness into an open-neighborhood obstruction, or prove that it remains only a planar certificate.

source_refs.excerpt: only a single special-case certificate and cannot substitute for the open generic-class claim

open_problems.why_unsolved: The prior derivation has a concrete two-shock trigonometric gap, but reviewers treated it as a special case rather than a robust population frontier. The missing step is to show whether small perturbations of rows, signs, cumulants, and dimension preserve the support-gap mechanism.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: An open-neighborhood stability theorem around the planar witness, or a counter-classification showing which additional dimensions or restriction patterns destroy the witness mechanism.

literature_map:
1. Jorda 2005 introduced LP impulse-response regressions as direct horizon-by-horizon estimates with misspecification appeal.
2. Plagborg-Moller and Wolf 2021 is the core equivalence result: LPs and VARs share impulse-response estimands when restrictions target the same object.
3. Montiel Olea and Plagborg-Moller 2021 sharpen the LP side through robust inference under persistence and long horizons.
4. Montiel Olea, Plagborg-Moller, Qian, and Wolf 2024/2026 add local VAR misspecification results: LP intervals can keep coverage where short/moderate VARs undercover.
5. Gourieroux, Monfort, and Renne 2017 and Lanne, Meitz, and Saikkonen 2017 use independent non-Gaussian shocks to identify SVAR rotations and impulse responses.
6. Guay 2021 gives higher-unconditional-moment rank conditions and tests, including partial local identification.
7. Lanne and Luoto 2021 supply a GMM non-Gaussian SVAR estimator, clarifying the nearest GMM comparator.
8. Drautzburg and Wright 2023 refine sign-restricted VAR sets by independence and emphasize weak higher-moment information and nonconvex sets.
9. Lanne, Liu, and Luoto 2026 relax independence toward potentially dependent non-Gaussian shocks, leaving active questions about set-identified blocks.
10. Arias, Rubio-Ramirez, and Waggoner 2018 and Read 2022 give sign/zero SVAR rotation geometry and algorithms, but not cumulant-distance LP/SVAR support bounds.
11. The live tension is that LP/VAR equivalence is a common-restriction theorem, whereas non-Gaussian SVAR restrictions are single-innovation-law restrictions.
12. No scanned paper gives a sharp algebraic boundary for LP sign/zero response sets versus non-Gaussian cumulant-diagonalizing SVAR response sets.
prior_proposal_map:
1. Walked the parent bank README first; the live parent entry is CausalSmith/doc/formalization/_bank/downgraded/eid_lp_svar_nonequiv_v1/README.md, while no accepted-tier parent README exists in this tree.
2. The v1 proposal, rejected-angle files, derivation, and reviewer JSONs all point to the same unresolved kernel: strict LP/sign versus non-Gaussian SVAR/sign separation was assumed or witnessed, not derived generically.
3. Reviewer JSON strengthening_paths yielded the highest-value gaps: computable support-gap diagnostic, rank/transversality separation, corrected Guay/Lanne-Luoto comparison, and equal-kurtosis boundary handling.
4. Honest scope flags in the v1 proposal say the primitive open-class separation and two-shock witness remain unproved.
5. The bank README states that no closed-form axis-b non-equivalence threshold was derived.
6. Adjacent reusable catalogue motifs named by the prior run are q1_spectral_phase_transition for row-space/spectral boundaries and pid_network_exposure / pid_dose_response_iv for shared-completion or common-latent pasting.
7. Study-pipeline gaps were checked as augmenting candidates, but their LATE/Manski interval-regression focus is off-anchor for this LP/SVAR non-Gaussian run.
